__inputs.name: DS_PROMETHEUS
__inputs.label: Prometheus
__inputs.description: Prometheus data source scraping the OTLP -> Prometheus stream that carries mdk's OTel metrics (same source as the #518 golden-signal dashboards).

__inputs.name: DS_INSIGHTS
__inputs.label: Insights API (JSON / Infinity)
__inputs.description: JSON/Infinity datasource pointed at the ADR 047 Observability Intelligence API (GET /api/v1/observability/insights + /health). Feeds the fleet-health gauge, the wins/risks lists, and the narrative. Plugin: yesoreyeram-infinity-datasource. NOTE: ADR 047 is not on main yet -- those panels render their documented-gap state until it ships (see dashboards/grafana/insights/README.md).

list.name: CalVer deploys
list.$comment: CalVer (YYYY.M.D.N) deploy markers from the ADR 047 insights digest. Lets leadership see a spend/latency step-change line up with the release that caused it. mdk demo seed emits deploy events with the same shape so the marker shows during a demo once ADR 047 is wired.

description: mdk - executive summary (single pane). The one screen leadership looks at: fleet health, spend-this-period + forecast, adoption, top wins/risks, and SLO error budget. Business-framed, not SRE-framed -- few big panels, exec-glance layout. Spend, forecast, runs, error-budget come from the real OTel metrics (mdk.run.cost_usd, mdk.jobs.completed) on Prometheus; health/wins/risks come from the ADR 047 insights API (scaffold-with-note until that ships). Styled with the Movate palette (dashboards/grafana/theme/). For the deep-dive SRE view see mdk-golden-signals.json (#518); for the narrative view see insights/mdk-daily-insights.json (#555).

options.content:
# Movate MDK — Executive Summary

**The one screen.** Fleet health, spend + forecast, adoption, wins/risks, and SLO error budget for the selected period. Business-framed — the SRE deep-dive is one click away (top-right links). Spend, runs, and error-budget are live from the OTel metrics; **fleet health and the wins/risks lists come from the ADR 047 insights API** (they show a documented-gap state until that endpoint ships — see the note at the bottom). Period selector: top-right time picker (defaults to this month).

panels.title: Fleet health
panels.description: Composite fleet health_score (0-100) from GET /api/v1/observability/health -- the ADR 047 blend of success rate, latency-vs-baseline, cost-vs-baseline, and eval pass-rate. Worst-of or fleet-mean per the API. Thresholds: red <50, amber 50-79, green >=80. SCAFFOLD-WITH-NOTE: until ADR 047 is on main this renders empty; the metric below (live success rate) is the closest standalone proxy.

panels.title: Live success rate (proxy)
panels.description: Live fleet success rate from mdk.jobs.completed -- the standalone proxy for fleet health while the ADR 047 health_score gauge (left) is awaiting its endpoint. success / all over the dashboard window. Thresholds match the golden-signal error SLO (10% error => 90% success).

panels.title: Runs this period + WoW
panels.description: Adoption headline: total runs (mdk.jobs.completed) over the period, with the week-over-week trend sparkline. The WoW delta badge comes from the same counter compared to the prior 7d window.

panels.title: Spend this period
panels.description: Total LLM spend (mdk.run.cost_usd) over the dashboard period. The headline cost number for leadership. Pairs with the forecast panel to the right.

panels.title: Spend forecast vs budget (end-of-period projection)
panels.description: Cumulative spend (mdk.run.cost_usd) with a linear projection (predict_linear over 30d) extrapolated to the period end, against the $MONTHLY_BUDGET line. The classic 'at this rate you'll hit budget on day X' exec view. See dashboards/grafana/theme/cost-forecast-panel.json for the reusable snippet + the methodology note.

options.content:
**Spend** = `mdk.run.cost_usd` over the period.

**Forecast** = `predict_linear` over the last 7d, extrapolated 30d. The dashed amber line is where spend lands at the current rate.

**Budget** = the `$monthly_budget` dashboard variable (top of screen). When the dashed line crosses the red budget line, that's your projected breach date.

> Cost is **observed, not paged** (ADR 017 — no cost SLO alert). This panel is the leadership early-warning, not an alert.

Reusable snippet: `theme/cost-forecast-panel.json`.

panels.title: SLO error budget — burndown (this period)
panels.description: Google-SRE error-budget burndown. SLO = 99% success (1% error budget). This plots error-budget CONSUMED as a fraction of the period's allowance: observed errors / (allowed-error-rate * total runs). 1.0 = budget exhausted (red line). Keyed to mdk.jobs.completed. See theme/slo-error-budget-panel.json for the reusable snippet + how to retune the SLO target.

panels.title: Error budget remaining
panels.description: 1 - (error budget consumed), floored at 0. The single number leadership wants: how much of this period's reliability allowance is left. SLO target = $slo_target (default 99%). Green when comfortable, red when exhausted.

options.content:
**SLO** = `$slo_target` success (default **99%**), so the budget is **1% of runs may fail** this period.

**Consumed** = observed errors / allowed errors. **1.0 = budget gone**.

**Remaining** (gauge) = `1 - consumed`, floored at 0.

| Remaining | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| green >50% | comfortable |
| amber 25-50% | slow down risky changes |
| red <25% | freeze; protect reliability |

Retune the target with the `$slo_target` variable. Reusable snippet: `theme/slo-error-budget-panel.json`.

panels.title: Top 3 wins
panels.description: The period's three best outcomes from GET /api/v1/observability/insights (wins[]): canary promotions, pass-rate gains, cost savings. SCAFFOLD-WITH-NOTE: wins[] is delivered by ADR 047 (not on main yet). mdk demo seed emits canary_promotion events shaped for this feed so the panel populates in a demo once ADR 047 is wired.

panels.title: Top 3 risks
panels.description: The period's three biggest risks from GET /api/v1/observability/insights (risks[] -- merged anomalies + drift + top failures, by severity). SCAFFOLD-WITH-NOTE: delivered by ADR 047 (not on main yet). mdk demo seed emits cost_anomaly / latency_anomaly / drift_detected events shaped for this feed.

options.content:
**Live today** (real OTel metrics on Prometheus, #518 catalog): Spend this period, Spend forecast vs budget, Runs + WoW, Live success rate, SLO error-budget burndown + remaining. Run `mdk demo seed` to light these up with a believable fleet.

**Scaffold-with-note** (awaiting **ADR 047** Observability Intelligence API, not on `main` yet): Fleet health gauge, Top 3 wins, Top 3 risks. These read `GET /api/v1/observability/{health,insights}` via the Infinity datasource; until ADR 047 ships they render empty (Grafana shows a datasource note on those panels). The seeder emits `deploy` / `canary_promotion` / `cost_anomaly` / `latency_anomaly` / `drift_detected` events shaped for the wins/risks feeds, so they populate end-to-end the moment ADR 047 lands.

**Active-agent count** is intentionally not a panel here: `mdk.run.cost_usd` / `mdk.jobs.completed` carry only `tenant` (not `agent`) per the catalog (`docs/observability.md`), so a fleet-wide distinct-agent count isn't available metric-side — it belongs to the ADR 047 health payload (`active_agents`). Documented gap, not a bug.

Palette + kiosk variant: `dashboards/grafana/theme/`.

title: mdk - executive summary
